title: Accountants and Auditors
description: Examine, analyze, and interpret accounting records to prepare financial statements, give advice, or audit and evaluate statements prepared by others. Install or advise on systems of recording costs or other financial and budgetary data.

profiles.description: Prepare detailed reports on audit findings.
profiles.rationale: Requires professional judgment and liability to “prepare detailed reports on audit findings,” so a human must sign off; IIA/AICPA audit lifecycle guidance indicates reporting often consumes several hours (e.g., ~6 hours) per completed engagement.

profiles.description: Report to management about asset utilization and audit results, and recommend changes in operations and financial activities.
profiles.rationale: The task involves presenting “asset utilization and audit results” and to “recommend changes,” which is a professional advisory deliverable requiring human authority; IIA Standards on communicating results suggest dedicated hours for management reporting in each audit.

profiles.description: Collect and analyze data to detect deficient controls, duplicated effort, extravagance, fraud, or non-compliance with laws, regulations, and management policies.
profiles.rationale: Analyzing data to detect “deficient controls… fraud, or non-compliance” can be AI-assisted for data prep/analytics but requires human evaluation of red flags; IIA internal audit time studies place significant hours (~1 workday+) on fieldwork analytics per engagement.

profiles.description: Inspect account books and accounting systems for efficiency, effectiveness, and use of accepted accounting procedures to record transactions.
profiles.rationale: “Inspect account books and accounting systems” can be partially automated with data extraction/testing but conclusions about “efficiency” and GAAP use need human judgment; IIA guidance shows substantial fieldwork hours per audit for controls/testing.

profiles.description: Supervise auditing of establishments, and determine scope of investigation required.
profiles.rationale: To “supervise auditing… and determine scope” entails accountable scoping and oversight decisions that must be made by a licensed professional; IIA practice guides note audit planning/supervision requires dedicated human hours per engagement.

profiles.description: Confer with company officials about financial and regulatory matters.
profiles.rationale: “Confer with company officials about financial and regulatory matters” is a sensitive, live consultation requiring human presence and responsibility; AICPA client-engagement norms anticipate hour-long meetings for such discussions.

profiles.description: Examine and evaluate financial and information systems, recommending controls to ensure system reliability and data integrity.
profiles.rationale: Evaluating “financial and information systems” and “recommending controls” can leverage AI for documentation review and control mapping but requires human assessment; IIA/IS audit practices allocate several hours to control evaluation per engagement.

profiles.description: Inspect cash on hand, notes receivable and payable, negotiable securities, and canceled checks to confirm records are accurate.
profiles.rationale: “Inspect cash on hand… negotiable securities” involves physical verification and custody-sensitive procedures that an AI cannot execute; AICPA audit procedures (cash counts/confirmations) typically take tens of minutes per location/account.

profiles.description: Examine records and interview workers to ensure recording of transactions and compliance with laws and regulations.
profiles.rationale: This requires reviewing records and “interview[ing] workers… [for] compliance,” a direct human interaction and compliance assessment with legal implications; IIA standards emphasize auditor-led interviews often take about an hour each.

profiles.description: Prepare, examine, or analyze accounting records, financial statements, or other financial reports to assess accuracy, completeness, and conformance to reporting and procedural standards.
profiles.rationale: Preparing/examining “financial statements” for “accuracy… conformance” can be drafted by AI but needs human review and sign-off; IMA finance productivity benchmarks show several hours per close/reporting cycle for this work.

profiles.description: Prepare adjusting journal entries.
profiles.rationale: “Prepare adjusting journal entries” can be proposed by AI from variance rules but still requires human approval; IMA/APQC accounting benchmarks indicate ~5–15 minutes per JE in typical organizations.

profiles.description: Review accounts for discrepancies and reconcile differences.
profiles.rationale: “Review accounts for discrepancies and reconcile differences” can be AI-assisted via automated matching but final exceptions require human judgment; IMA account reconciliation benchmarks suggest ~30 minutes per straightforward account.

profiles.description: Review taxpayer accounts, and conduct audits on-site, by correspondence, or by summoning taxpayer to office.
profiles.rationale: To “conduct audits on-site… or by summoning taxpayer” carries legal/authority requirements and often involves fieldwork; AICPA/Thomson Reuters tax practice benchmarks show full-day efforts per audit case are common.

profiles.description: Examine inventory to verify journal and ledger entries.
profiles.rationale: “Examine inventory to verify journal and ledger entries” can use AI to cross-check records but a human confirms conclusions; AICPA/IIA inventory audit procedures suggest short, repeated checks per item/category.

profiles.description: Establish tables of accounts and assign entries to proper accounts.
profiles.rationale: Designing a “table of accounts” and assigning entries benefits from AI templates but still needs human design choices; IMA management accounting guidance notes hours of setup per implementation.

profiles.description: Compute taxes owed and prepare tax returns, ensuring compliance with payment, reporting, or other tax requirements.
profiles.rationale: “Compute taxes owed and prepare tax returns” can be drafted by AI/tax software but requires a preparer’s review and signature; AICPA/Thomson Reuters tax workflow benchmarks cite ~3 hours for typical returns.

profiles.description: Advise clients in areas such as compensation, employee health care benefits, the design of accounting or data processing systems, or long-range tax or estate plans.
profiles.rationale: To “advise clients” on benefits, systems, or “long-range tax or estate plans” involves fiduciary, personalized judgment in live consultation; AICPA advisory practice norms assume ~1-hour consultations.

profiles.description: Analyze business operations, trends, costs, revenues, financial commitments, and obligations to project future revenues and expenses or to provide advice.
profiles.rationale: Analyzing operations to “project future revenues and expenses” can be modeled by AI but requires human validation and communication; IMA FP&A benchmarks show several hours per forecasting cycle.

profiles.description: Report to management regarding the finances of establishment.
profiles.rationale: Standardized “report to management regarding the finances” can be auto-generated from ERP dashboards with minimal review; IMA reporting benchmarks indicate weekly management packs take 1–2 hours manually.

profiles.description: Develop, implement, modify, and document recordkeeping and accounting systems, making use of current computer technology.
profiles.rationale: “Develop, implement, modify, and document” accounting systems can be scaffolded by AI but needs human-led configuration and controls design; IMA/ACCA digital finance transformation guidance shows multi-hour efforts per project.

profiles.description: Evaluate taxpayer finances to determine tax liability, using knowledge of interest and discount rates, annuities, valuation of stocks and bonds, and amortization valuation of depletable assets.
profiles.rationale: Evaluating taxpayer finances to “determine tax liability” can be computed by AI but must be reviewed by a professional; AICPA Tax Section resources indicate hours per return for liability determination.

profiles.description: Direct activities of personnel engaged in filing, recording, compiling, and transmitting financial records.
profiles.rationale: To “direct activities of personnel” is people management requiring human oversight and accountability; IMA management accounting studies note routine supervisory time blocks weekly.

profiles.description: Examine whether the organization's objectives are reflected in its management activities, and whether employees understand the objectives.
profiles.rationale: Assessing whether objectives are “reflected in… activities” involves analysis and staff understanding checks that AI can prepare but humans must conclude; IIA performance auditing guidance allocates hours for this evaluative work.

profiles.description: Conduct pre-implementation audits to determine if systems and programs under development will work as planned.
profiles.rationale: “Pre-implementation audits” of systems can be supported by AI for document review/testing plans but require human assurance judgments; IIA/IS audit practices cite multiple hours for pre-implementation reviews.

profiles.description: Audit payroll and personnel records to determine unemployment insurance premiums, workers' compensation coverage, liabilities, and compliance with tax laws.
profiles.rationale: Auditing payroll to determine liabilities and “compliance with tax laws” entails legal compliance assessment and sign-off; AICPA/IIA compliance audit guidance indicates sustained human effort per audit area.

profiles.description: Develop, maintain, or analyze budgets, preparing periodic reports that compare budgeted costs to actual costs.
profiles.rationale: “Prepare… periodic reports that compare budgeted costs to actuals” is highly standardized and can be automated; IMA FP&A benchmarks show recurring variance reports typically consume about an hour manually.

profiles.description: Prepare, analyze, or verify annual reports, financial statements, and other records, using accepted accounting and statistical procedures to assess financial condition and facilitate financial planning.
profiles.rationale: Preparing/verifying “annual reports [and] financial statements” can be drafted by AI tools but requires CPA review for compliance; AICPA financial reporting practice guides indicate many hours per annual close/report.

profiles.description: Process invoices for payment.
profiles.rationale: “Process invoices for payment” fits straight-through AP automation with minimal spot-checks; IMA/APQC AP benchmarks report ~5 minutes per invoice manually.

profiles.description: Review data about material assets, net worth, liabilities, capital stock, surplus, income, or expenditures.
profiles.rationale: “Review data about… assets… liabilities… expenditures” is standardized data compilation that AI can automate; IMA finance analytics benchmarks place such reviews in short, repeatable tasks.
